NOTICE OF CHANGE OF ADDRESS FILED BY ATTORNEY HERNAEZ, ALEX

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY LITIGATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT WAIVED THE RIGHT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT UNREASONABLY DELAYED BRINGING THIS MOTION, THE PARTIES HAVE ALREADY EXCHANGED DISCOVERY REQUESTS, AND DEFENDANT WAS ALREADY DEPOSED. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)
REPLY TO PLAINTIFF SHEILA SCOTTS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SANCTIONS (CCP SECTION 128.7) (TRANSACTION ID # 100005268) FILED BY DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC
LAW AND MOTION, 302, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL, IS ADVANCED FROM NOV-17-2015 TO LAW AND MOTION ON NOV-06-2015 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, PER THE EX PARTE ORDER FILED ON 10/19/15.
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED ADVANCING TIME FOR BIFURCATION MOTION HEARING GRANTED BY THE COURT (TRANSACTION ID # 100007248) FILED BY DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE THE DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S ENTITLEMENT TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND THE AMOUNT TO BE AWARDED FROM THE LIABILITY PHASE IS GRANTED. IN PRACTICE, WHEN A TRIAL IS BIFURCATED UNDER A STATUTE REQUIRING THAT EVIDENCE OF A DEFENDANT'S FINANCIAL CONDITION BE PRESENTED ONLY AFTER THE DEFENDANT IS FOUND LIABLE FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, ALL EVIDENCE RELATING TO THE AMOUNT OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS OFFERED IN THE SECOND PHASE, WHILE THE DETERMINATION OF WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DECIDED IN THE FIRST PHASE ALONG WITH THE COMPENSATORY DAMAGES. (HOLDGRAFER V. UNOCAL. CORPS. (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 907, 919; WEST'S ANN.CAL.CIV.CODE 3294, 3295.) HERE, HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE BIFURCATION OF THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES PORTION OF THE CLAIM. (PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION PAGE 2: 10-11.) THE MOTION TO BIFURCATE THE DETERMINATION OF COMPENSATORY DAMAGES FROM THE DETERMINATION OF LIABILITY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THAT IT WILL BE PREJUDICED OR THAT THE COURT WILL BE INCONVENIENCED IF THE COURT DOES NOT DEVIATE FROM THE NORMAL PRACTICE OF ALLOWING THE DETERMINATION OF LIABILITY AND COMPENSATORY DAMAGES TO BE MADE TOGETHER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial
Off Calendar Oct-19-2015 Advanced From Nov-17-15 To Nov-06-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-26-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial
Off Calendar Nov-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC's Motion To Bifurcate Trial - Motion granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's motion to bifurcate the determination of Plaintiff's entitlement to punitive damages and the amount to be awarded from the liability phase is granted. In practice, when a trial is bifurcated under a statute requiring that evidence of a defendant's financial condition be presented only after the defendant is found liable for punitive damages, all evidence relating to the amount of punitive damages is offered in the second phase, while the determination of whether the plaintiff is entitled to punitive damages is decided in the first phase along with the compensatory damages. (Holdgrafer v. Unocal. Corps. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 907, 919; West's Ann.Cal.Civ.Code 3294, 3295.) Here, however, Plaintiff does not oppose bifurcation of the punitive damages portion of the claim. (Plaintiff's Opposition page 2: 10-11.) The motion to bifurcate the determination of compensatory damages from the determination of liability is denied. Defendant has not submitted sufficient evidence to establish that it will be prejudiced or that the court will be inconvenienced if the court does not deviate from the normal practice of allowing the determination of liability and compensatory damages to be made together. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAFAEL CRESPO, TEL. NO. 1-415-285-2063 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTINA S. CHEN OF THE FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-364-5523 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S Motion For Sanctions is denied. First, it is unclear whether defendant complied with the safe harbor provisions of CCP 128.7 and gave plaintiff an opportunity to withdraw or amend the pleading. See CCP 128.7(c)(1). Second, defendant fails to show that the allegations are frivolous as opposed to an honest mistake. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation is denied. Defendant waived the right to compel arbitration. Defendant unreasonably delayed bringing this motion, the parties have already exchanged discovery requests, and defendant was already deposed. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
